advancement.twilightforest.kill_lich.des: Slay the %s at top of his tower and retrieve a Mechanical Tool to clear poisonous mosquitoes from the Swamp, see through blinding darkness of the Dark Forest, and resist the Snowy Forest's chill
advancement.twilightforest.lich_scepters: Acquire all Mechanical Tools of power
advancement.twilightforest.progress_knig: Settle the restless phantoms in the knight's tomb and the carminite tower's devices will obey you
advancement.twilightforest.progression_e: Anything past this point in the Highlands is a work in progress. It will be finished in the future (INSHAALLAH)
block.minecraft.banner.twilightforest.li: Light Blue Twilight Monster King Crown
Light Gray Twilight Monster King Crown
Shift + right-click the same block to place finial
config.twilightforest.animate_trophies.t: Rotate Trophy heads on item model. Has no performance impact at all. For those who don't like fun.
config.twilightforest.aurora_biomes.tool: Defines which biomes the aurora shader effect will appear in. Leave the list empty to disable the effect.
config.twilightforest.boss_drop_chests.t:
If true, Twilight Forest's bosses will put their drops inside of a chest where they originally spawned instead of dropping the loot directly.
Note that the Knight Phantoms are not affected by this as their drops work differently.
config.twilightforest.casket_uuid_lockin:
If true, Keepsake Caskets that are spawned when a player dies will not be accessible by other players. Use this if you dont want people taking from other people's death caskets.
NOTE: server operators will still be able to open locked caskets.
config.twilightforest.check_portal_place:
Determines if new portals should be pre-checked for safety. If false, portals will fail to form rather than redirect to a safe alternate destination.
Note that disabling this also reduces the rate at which portal formation checks are performed.
config.twilightforest.cloud_precipitatio:
Dictates how many blocks down from a cloud block should the game logic check for handling weather related code.
Lower if experiencing low tick rate. Set to 0 to turn all cloud precipitation logic off.
config.twilightforest.default_item_encha:
If false, items that come enchanted when you craft them (such as ironwood or steeleaf gear) will not show this way in the creative inventory.
Please note that this doesn't affect the crafting recipes themselves, you will need a datapack to change those.
config.twilightforest.disable_uncrafting:
Disables the uncrafting function of the uncrafting table. Recommended as a last resort if there's too many things to change about its behavior (or you're just lazy, I dont judge).
Do note that special uncrafting recipes are not disabled as the mod relies on them for other things.

Disables any usage of the uncrafting table, as well as prevents it from showing up in loot or crafted.
Please note that table has more uses than just uncrafting, you can read about them here! http://benimatic.com/tfwiki/index.php?title=Uncrafting_Table
It is highly recommended to keep the table enabled as the mod has special uncrafting exclusive recipes, but the option remains for people that dont want the table to be functional at all.
If you are looking to just prevent normal crafting recipes from being reversed, consider using the 'disableUncrafting' option instead.
config.twilightforest.giant_skin_uuid_li: List of player UUIDs whose skins the Giants should use. Leave the list empty to use the skin of the player viewing them.
config.twilightforest.ingredient_switchi:
If true, the uncrafting table will no longer allow you to switch between ingredients if a recipe uses a tag for crafting.
This will remove the functionality for ALL RECIPES!
If you want to prevent certain ingredients from showing up in the first place, use the "twilightforest:banned_uncrafting_ingredients" tag.
config.twilightforest.locked_toasts.tool: Disables the toasts that appear when entering a locked biome. Not recommended if you're unfamiliar with progression.
config.twilightforest.magic_trees.toolti: Settings for all things related to the magic trees.
config.twilightforest.max_portal_size.to: The max amount of water spaces the mod will check for when creating a portal. Very high numbers may cause performance issues.
config.twilightforest.mining_range.toolt:
Defines the radius at which the Minewood Core works. Can be a number anywhere between 1 and 128.
Set to 0 to prevent the Minewood Core from functioning.
config.twilightforest.multiplayer_fight_:
Determines how bosses should adjust to multiplayer fights. There are 4 possible values that can be put here:
NONE: doesnt do anything when multiple people participate in a bossfight. Bosses will act the same as they do in singleplayer or solo fights.
MORE_LOOT: adds additional drops to a boss' loot table based on how many players participated in the fight. These are fully controlled through the entity's loot table, using the `twilightforest:multiplayer_multiplier` loot function. Note that this function will only do things to entities that are included in the `twilightforest:multiplayer_inclusive_entities` tag.
MORE_HEALTH: increases the health of each boss by 20 hearts for each player nearby when the fight starts.
MORE_LOOT_AND_HEALTH: does both of the above functions for each boss.
config.twilightforest.optifine.tooltip: Disable the nag screen when OptiFine is installed.
config.twilightforest.origin_dimension.t: The dimension you can always travel to the Twilight Forest from, as well as the dimension you will return to. Defaults to the overworld. (domain:regname).
config.twilightforest.parry_non_twilight: Set to true to parry non-Twilight projectiles.
config.twilightforest.parry_window.toolt: The amount of ticks after raising a shield that makes it OK to parry a projectile. (1 tick = 1/20 second)
config.twilightforest.portal_for_new_pla: If true, the return portal will spawn for new players that were sent to the TF if `newPlayersSpawnInTF` is true.
Allows people with the specified permission or higher to create portals. This is based off of Vanilla's permission system.
You can read about them here: https://minecraft.wiki/w/Permission_level
config.twilightforest.portal_return.tool: If false, the return portal will require the activation item.
config.twilightforest.portal_settings.to: All settings regarding the Twilight Forest Portal are found here
config.twilightforest.portal_unlocked_by: Use a valid advancement resource location as a string. For example, using the string "minecraft:story/mine_diamond" will lock the portal behind the "Diamonds!" advancement. Invalid/Empty Advancement resource IDs will leave the portal entirely unlocked.
config.twilightforest.portals_in_other_d: Allow portals to the Twilight Forest to be made outside of the 'origin' dimension. May be considered an exploit.
config.twilightforest.prettify_ore_meter: Lines up the dashes and percentages in the Ore Meter GUI.
config.twilightforest.ram_indicator.tool: Renders a check mark or X above your crosshair while holding wool when hovering over the Questing Ram depending on if that color of wool has been fed to it already.
Multiplies the total XP cost of repairing an item and rounds up.
Higher values means the recipe will cost more to repair, lower means less. Set to 0 to disable the cost altogether.
config.twilightforest.screen_shake.toolt: Controls whether the screen shakes when a Magic Bean is in the process of growing.
config.twilightforest.shapeless_uncrafti:
If true, the uncrafting table will also be allowed to uncraft shapeless recipes.
The table was originally intended to only take shaped recipes, but this option remains for people who wish to keep the functionality.
config.twilightforest.shield.tooltip: We recommend downloading the Shield Parry mod for parrying, but these controls remain for without.
config.twilightforest.shield_indicator.t:
Renders how many fortification shields are currently active on your player above your armor bar.
"Turn this off if other mods render over/under it.
config.twilightforest.shield_indicator_c: Enables the fortification shield indicator in creative for debugging.
config.twilightforest.silent_cicadas.too: Makes Cicadas silent for those having sound library problems or finding them annoying.
config.twilightforest.silent_cicadas_on_: Makes Cicadas silent when one is on your head. If the above option is set to true, this won't have any effect.
config.twilightforest.sorting_range.tool:
Defines the radius at which the Sortingwood Core works. Can be a number anywhere between 1 and 128.
Set to 0 to prevent the Sortingwood Core from functioning.
config.twilightforest.spawn_in_tf.toolti: If true, players spawning for the first time will spawn in the Twilight Forest.
config.twilightforest.time_range.tooltip:
Defines the radius at which the Timewood Core works. Can be a number anywhere between 1 and 128.
Set to 0 to prevent the Timewood Core from functioning.
config.twilightforest.totem_charm_animat: Makes Charms when triggered display like the Totem of Undying instead of our own effects.
config.twilightforest.transformation_ran:
Defines the radius at which the Transformation Core works. Can be a number anywhere between 1 and 128.
Set to 0 to prevent the Transformation Core from functioning.
config.twilightforest.uncrafting.tooltip: Settings for all things related to the Uncrafting Table.
config.twilightforest.uncrafting_mod_id_: If true, this will invert the above option from a blacklist to a whitelist.
config.twilightforest.uncrafting_mod_ids:
Here, you can disable all items from certain mods from being uncrafted.
Input a valid mod id to disable all uncrafting recipes from that mod.
Example: "twilightforest" will disable all uncrafting recipes from this mod.
config.twilightforest.uncrafting_recipes:
If you don't want to disable uncrafting altogether, and would rather disable certain recipes, this is for you.
To add a recipe, add the mod id followed by the name of the recipe. You can check this in things like JEI.
Example: "twilightforest:firefly_particle_spawner" will disable uncrafting the particle spawner into a firefly jar, firefly, and poppy.
If an item has multiple crafting recipes and you wish to disable them all, add the item to the "twilightforest:banned_uncraftables" item tag.
If you have a problematic ingredient, like infested towerwood for example, add the item to the "twilightforest:banned_uncrafting_ingredients" item tag.
If true, this will invert the above uncrafting recipe list from a blacklist to a whitelist.
config.twilightforest.uncrafting_xp_cost:
Multiplies the total XP cost of uncrafting an item and rounds up.
Higher values means the recipe will cost more to uncraft, lower means less. Set to 0 to disable the cost altogether.
Note that this only affects reversed crafting recipes, uncrafting recipes will still use the same cost as they normally would.

enchantment.twilightforest.renewal.desc: Automatically recharges Mechanical Tools when they run out if the holder has the recharge items in their inventory.
gamerule.tfEnforcedProgression.descripti: Some Twilight Forest biomes will be locked until you defeat certain bosses in the dimension. (You can check the progression order in your advancements)
gui.twilightforest.optifine.message:
Before proceeding, please note that Optifine is known to cause crashes, multipart entity visual bugs and many other issues.

Before reporting a bug, please remove Optifine first and check again to see if the bug is still present.

Optifine-related issues are not solvable on Twilight Forest's end!

This screen may be disabled in the Client Config.
gui.twilightforest.optifine.suggestions: Here's a selection of mods that we recommend using instead.
gui.twilightforest.optifine.title: WARNING: OPTIFINE DETECTED
gui.twilightforest.progression_end.messa: This is the end of progression for now. The Final Castle that awaits on the plateau is still unfinished and a work in progress. If you would like to keep up with the mod's development you can join our %s.
item.twilightforest.block_and_chain_gobl: Block and Chain Goblin Spawn Egg
item.twilightforest.lich_spawn_egg: Twilight Twilight Monster King Spawn Egg
item.twilightforest.pocket_watch.desc: It seems to always be running late
misc.twilightforest.magic_map_fail: The Map faltered. Perhaps it doesn't work here?
subtitles.twilightforest.entity.lich_clo: Twilight Monster King clone ignores attack
subtitles.twilightforest.entity.minion.s: Twilight Monster King summons new minion
twilightforest.book.darktower.1:
§8[[An explorer's notebook that seems to have survived an explosion]]§0

This tower clearly has mechanisms that are not responding to me. Their magic almost yearns to acknowledge my touch, but it cannot. It is if the devices of the tower are being
twilightforest.book.darktower.2:
suppressed by a powerful group of beings nearby.

§8[[Next entry]]§0

The magic seems to emanate from deep within the strongholds nearby. It can't come from the goblins, as their magic is charming, but unfocused. There
twilightforest.book.darktower.3:
must still be some force still active in the strongholds.

§8[[Next entry]]§0

My analysis indicates that it comes from several sources, operating as a group. I will head back to the stronghold after I resupply...
twilightforest.book.hydralair.1:
§8[[An explorer's notebook, written on fireproof paper]]§0

Fire is a trivial obstacle for a master explorer such as myself. I have traversed seas of fire, and swam through oceans of lava. The burning air here is an interesting variation, but
twilightforest.book.hydralair.2:
ultimately no hindrance.

What does stop me though is that I have encountered another protection spell, this time surrounding a mighty creature that must be king of this fire swamp. This is not the first protection spell I have encountered, and I am
twilightforest.book.hydralair.3:
beginning to unravel the mysteries of how they work.

If this spell is like the others, it will be sustained by a powerful creature nearby. Surrounding the fire swamp are several wet swamps, and under those swamps are labyrinths full of minotaurs.
twilightforest.book.hydralair.4: The logical choice to bind such a spell to would be some sort of powerful minotaur, different in some way from the others that surround it...
twilightforest.book.icetower.1:
§8[[An explorer's notebook, caked in ice]]§0

I overcame one blizzard, only to run into this terrible ice storm atop the glacier. My explorations have shown me the splendor of an ice palace, shining with the colors of the
twilightforest.book.icetower.2:
polar aurora. It all seems protected by some sort of curse.

§8[[Next entry]]§0

I am no novice. This curse is fed by the power of a creature nearby. The cause of the curse surrounding the fire swamp was built off the power of the
twilightforest.book.icetower.3:
leader of the minotaurs nearby.

Surrounding this glacier, there are masses of yetis. Perhaps the yetis have some sort of leader...
twilightforest.book.labyrinth.1:
§8[[An explorer's notebook, written on waterproof paper]]§0

The mosquitoes in this swamp are vexing, but strange. The vast majority of them seem to have no natural source, nor do they seem to have a role in the local ecology. I have begun to suspect that they are
twilightforest.book.labyrinth.2:
some kind of magical curse.

§8[[Next entry]]§0

Now that I have encountered a protection spell on the ruined labyrinth here, I consider my suspicions confirmed. Both the protection spell and the mosquitoes are a
twilightforest.book.labyrinth.3:
curse. This curse seems to have a different source from the others I have encountered. I will have to research further...

§8[[Next entry]]§0

The curse seems to be of a type too powerful for one being alone to
twilightforest.book.labyrinth.4:
produce. Several wizards working in combination would be necessary.

If one of the wizards stopped contributing, the whole of the curse over the entire swamp would fall. Strangely, my divinations do not show signs of any nearby living wizards.
twilightforest.book.labyrinth.5: I did see something interesting in one of the nearby pointy-roofed towers though...
twilightforest.book.lichtower.1:
§8[An explorer's notebook, gnawed on by monsters]§0

I have begun examining the strange aura surrounding this tower. The bricks of the tower are protected by a curse, stronger than any I've seen before. The magic from the curse is boiling off into the
twilightforest.book.lichtower.2:
surrounding area.

In my homeland I would have many options for dealing with this magic, but here my supplies are limited. I shall have to research...
twilightforest.book.lichtower.3:
§8[[Many entries later]]§0

A breakthrough! In my journeys I sighted a huge snake-like monster in a decorated courtyard. Nearby, I picked up a worn down, discarded green scale.

The magic in the scale seems to have the curse-breaking
twilightforest.book.lichtower.4: properties I need, but the magic is too dim. I may need to acquire a fresher specimen, directly from the creature.
twilightforest.book.tfstronghold.1:
§8[[An explorer's notebook, written on faintly glowing paper]]§0

The tendrils of darkness surrounding this area are just a manifestation of a protective spell over the entire dark forest. The spell causes blindness, which is quite vexing. I have seen several
twilightforest.book.tfstronghold.2:
interesting things in the area and would like to keep exploring.

§8[[Next entry]]§0

I have found ruins in the dark forest. They belong to a stronghold, of a type usually inhabited by knights. Rather than knights though, this stronghold is full of
twilightforest.book.tfstronghold.3:
goblins. They wear knightly armor, but their behavior is most un-knightly.

§8[[Next entry]]§0

Deep in the ruins, I have found a pedestal. The pedestal seems to be of a type that knights would place trophies on to prove their strength.
twilightforest.book.tfstronghold.4: Obtaining a powerful scepter would seem to weaken the blinding effect of the dark forest, and placing a trophy associated with a powerful creature on the pedestal would likely grant access into the main part of the stronghold.
twilightforest.book.trollcave.1:
§8[[An explorer's notebook, damaged by acid]]§0

There seems to be no way to protect myself from the toxic rainstorm surrounding this area. In my brief excursions, I have also encountered another protection spell, similar to the others I have
twilightforest.book.trollcave.2:
witnessed. The spell must be connected to the toxic storm in some way. Further research to follow...

§8[[Next entry]]§0

Such supreme weather magic must be the result of multiple undefeated great evils in this world. My studies contain
twilightforest.book.unknown.1:
§8[[This book shows signs of having been copied many times]]§0

I cannot explain the field surrounding this structure, but the magic is powerful. If this curse is like the others, then the answer to unlocking it lies elsewhere. Perhaps there is something I have left
twilightforest.book.unknown.2: undone, or some monster I have yet to defeat. I will have to turn back. I will return to this place later inshaAllah, to see if anything has changed.
twilightforest.book.yeticave.1:
§8[[An explorer's notebook, covered in frost]]§0

The blizzard surrounding these snowy lands is unceasing. This is no ordinary snowfall. I need to conduct experiments to find what is capable of
twilightforest.book.yeticave.2:
causing such an effect.

§8[[Next entry]]§0

The blizzard seems to be of a type too powerful for one being alone to produce. Several working in combination would be necessary. If one stopped
twilightforest.book.yeticave.3: contributing, the blizzard would calm. Strangely, my divinations do not show signs of anyone who could have caused this. Although, I did see something interesting in one of the nearby pointy-roofed towers...
twilightforest.tips.block_and_chain: Enknowledging a Block and Chain with Destruction allows it to break blocks.
twilightforest.tips.burnt_thorns: Burnt Thorns disintegrate when stepped on.
twilightforest.tips.charm_of_keeping: A Charm of Keeping will return parts of your inventory after death.
twilightforest.tips.charm_of_life: A Charm of Life can save you from a fatal blow.
twilightforest.tips.crumble_horn: The Crumble Horn will deteriorate nearby blocks when used.
twilightforest.tips.lich_scepters: The Twilight Monster King drops a variety of mechanical tools.
twilightforest.tips.magic_beans: Planting Magic Beans on Uberous Soil sprouts a mighty beanstalk.
twilightforest.tips.magic_leaves: Magic Tree Leaves won't drop saplings when broken.
twilightforest.tips.magic_map: Magic Maps are used to easily locate structures.
twilightforest.tips.magic_saplings: Special saplings can be found inside Hollow Oak trees.
twilightforest.tips.music_disc: Quran Discs are found outside of dungeons.
twilightforest.tips.transformation_tree: The Tree of Transformation will convert the area around it into an Twiknowledged Forest.
twilightforest.tips.zombie_healing: Green Monsters summoned with a Mechanical Tool of Green Monsters can be healed with Rotten Flesh.
